title: Implement generic finite field linear algebra (FieldMatrix)
description:
Generic dense and sparse linear algebra over arbitrary finite fields, built on `FieldVec<F: FiniteField>` from the GF(p^n) epic (e095a100).

## Primary goals

1. **UX ergonomics**: Clean, composable API that hides implementation complexity. Operator overloads (`*`, `+`), builder patterns, ergonomic indexing. Competing with Sage/Magma usability.
2. **Performance**: Delayed-reduction kernels, SIMD dispatch, blocked algorithms. Benchmark target: within 2× of fflas-ffpack for GF(p), exceed for GF(2^m).

## Scope

- `FieldMatrix<F: FiniteField>` dense matrix type (row-major over `FieldVec<F>`)
- Gaussian elimination, RREF, LU decomposition
- Matrix multiplication with delayed-reduction and Winograd for large matrices
- Matrix inversion, linear system solving (`Ax = b`)
- Sparse `FieldMatrix` (CSR/CSC) with SpMV
- Performance benchmarking against fflas-ffpack (mandatory gate)

## Architecture

- Lives in `crates/gf2-core/src/field/matrix.rs` (dense) and `field/sparse_matrix.rs` (sparse)
- `BitMatrix` remains the specialized GF(2) path — no changes to existing binary linear algebra
- Matrix multiply dispatches to `FieldVec::dot_product` as the inner kernel; SIMD and delayed reduction happen at that layer
- Blocked factorizations (LU → fgemm) follow the fflas-ffpack pattern for cache efficiency

## Non-goals

- Eigenvalue decomposition (not meaningful over finite fields)
- Floating-point BLAS integration (we use direct SIMD kernels)
- Replacing `BitMatrix` for GF(2) operations

## References

- [fflas-ffpack analysis](dev/plans/fflas_ffpack_analysis.md) — design patterns and benchmark methodology
